PLEASE FOR THE LOVE OF GOD SPOTIFY make your user interface updates actually improve the user experience — not worsen it.

Maybe I’m jaded, but I’ve been a Spotify user since 2009.

Year after year, the user interface has adjustments on mobile and desktop, and they are not to my benefit. They don’t reflect how I use the app and listen to music. 

I’ve been taking the -1HP as a user on this for years and years but I finally think I’m going to switch and use one of those sites that imports my music over to a different app.

I’ve only stayed because of the convenience of not switching my library over, however, the inconvenience of these updates has brought me to my end.

Please, someone tell me I’m not alone in this thinking. Or maybe I am and I’m just a legacy user and my time has come.

AI Generated Playlists

I've seen a lot of hate on the recent update that pushed out the "Create" button. Maybe it's not the best location for it, but I'm already finding the new AI playlist feature helpful!

I've been trying to get more into running and my main issue is that I'll start a run at too fast of a pace and tire out quickly. I've found 150 steps per minute is a good pace for me, so I've been creating a playlist of songs that are around 150 bpm. Today, I tried asking the AI to make me a playlist of 150 bpm rap workout songs, and it gave me a 3 hour playlist of songs (mostly) in the 140-160 bpm range. It's not perfect, but I'm probably adding 75% of the songs it gave me to my current playlist. I've been slowly adding to this playlist myself for a couple months now and I just doubled the amount of songs I had in there in about 15 minutes. 

  
So now I'm curious, has anyone else has found some creative uses for the AI playlists or other "create" features?
